ul.li:
AVX2 Alignment (ymm): Requires 32-byte alignment.
Using vmovaps on unaligned addresses causes #GP(0) fault.
Using vmovups (unaligned) is safer but slower if crossing lines.

AVX-512 Alignment (zmm): Requires 64-byte alignment.
Native width is 512-bits (64 bytes).
Alignment to 64B ensures the entire vector resides within a single cache line.

The Split Load Penalty:
When a load straddles two cache lines (Scenario B), the Load/Store Unit (LSU) must split the operation.
Latency: Costs 2+ extra cycles to merge results from two different L1D sets.
Throughput: Consumes two load ports instead of one, causing stalls in superscalar execution.

Implementation Strategy:
Allocators should use _mm_malloc(size, 64) or std::aligned_alloc(64, size).
Ensure row_pitch is a multiple of 64 bytes to maintain alignment across 2D structures.
